mnemonics: Euler's K-Circuits: Every Knight Prefers Riding Over Five Bridges (Eulerian circuits exist if all vertices have even degree, and a graph is k-connected if it remains connected after removing any k-1 vertices)
keyPoints: An Eulerian circuit exists in a connected graph if and only if every vertex has an even degree.
A graph is k-connected if the minimum number of vertices whose removal disconnects the graph is at least k.
Planar graphs can be drawn on a plane without any edges crossing, and Euler's Formula (V - E + F = 2) holds for connected planar graphs.
commonMistakes: Confusing Eulerian circuits (visits every edge once) with Hamiltonian circuits (visits every vertex once).
Assuming a graph is k-connected just because it has a high minimum degree (connectivity is about vertex removal, not degree).
Applying Euler's Formula (V - E + F = 2) to planar graphs that are not connected.
